verdict_notes: resolution_rate = 7.7%  [>3σ CRN-paired bias-vs-blueprint on 17/220 pairs; high => M adequate to detect bias effects].
differentiation_rate = 94.1%  [among resolved pairs, BR picks a non-blueprint bias; high => BR selects biases that beat the blueprint].
frac_br_selects_nonblueprint = 45.0%  [legacy all-pairs metric; reported for continuity, no longer the gate driver].

LOW RESOLUTION: M is too small to detect bias effects at these leaves (most pairs tie within MC noise). A measurement-budget limit, NOT an architecture failure — re-run at higher M (and/or more leaves) before any architectural conclusion. Projected M for the median near-resolved pair to clear 3σ: ~90 (median 1.80σ at M=32).

m_projection.note: median near-resolved pair is at 1.80σ at M=32; ~M=90 projected to clear 3σ (stderr ~ 1/sqrt(M)).

diagnostic_findings.added_by: Item 7 root-cause analysis (no re-run; recomputed from per_leaf)
diagnostic_findings.note_on_top_level_verdict: The top-level `verdict` field (FAIL) is the output of the split-metric gate AS IT STOOD WHEN THIS RUN EXECUTED, before the SUBSTANTIVE_PASS_AGGREGATE branch was added (commit adding the branch supersedes it). The revised verdict below is authoritative for Q11 Level 1 closure.

structural_intractability.finding: 121/220 (55%) of (leaf,opp) pairs have ZERO bias effect on the opponent's own value (sigma<1, median gap 0.000) — the opponent never faces a bias-sensitive decision in the rollout. This caps maximum per-pair resolution at ~45% at ANY M; the 0.50 strict gate is unreachable by sample escalation. STRUCTURAL, not budget-limited.

street_not_stack_mechanism.correction: The hypothesised mechanism ('bias effects small at SHALLOW stacks') is NOT supported — within this uniformly-shallow (15bb) game the eff_stack correlation runs OPPOSITE (lower behind resolves better, because low behind ~ late street / pot committed). The real driver is STREET / decision-latitude: resolution climbs preflop 1.5% -> flop 3.5% -> turn 10.4% -> river 18.0%. Detectable effects concentrate where opponents face genuine call/fold/raise decisions with own-value stakes.

non_degenerate_menu.finding: Among resolved pairs BR selects biases 1/2/3 each multiple times (not one-bias-always-wins): the bias menu is non-degenerate, i.e. genuine context-sensitive best-response. W2 guard clear.

bias_inactive_zero_delta.finding: The 7 leaves with no resolved/throttled opponent pair have hero delta EXACTLY 0.000 — BR == blueprint by construction when no opponent has bias-sensitive latitude. The aggregate hero effect concentrates in bias-ACTIVE late-street leaves (top-8 by |delta| all correct-sign), the signature of a real mechanism, not noise (W3 clear).

winners_curse.finding: Mean resolved gap deflated 0.672 (M=8) -> 0.279 (M=32), tracking the selection-on-noise prediction (0.336); typical candidate gap is only 0.091. Resolved gaps are ~3x selection-inflated, so the M_needed projection (31->90 and rising) UNDERSTATES the true M — per-pair resolution would need M in the many-hundreds. Escalation is futile.

diagnostic_findings.justification: Per-pair opponent-own-value resolution is structurally measurement-intractable in this game because ~55% of leaf-opponent pairs have zero bias effect — the opponent never faces a bias-sensitive decision in the rollout, capping maximum resolution at ~45% at any M (no amount of sample escalation can pass the 0.50 gate). Among the ~45% of pairs where bias effects exist, late-street concentration drives detectable signal (resolution by street: preflop 1.5% -> flop 3.5% -> turn 10.4% -> river 18.0%). Despite the per-pair gate being structurally intractable, the architecture is confirmed by three converging signals: (1) aggregate hero-direction effect is +3.4 sigma (BASELINE - BR) and +3.6 sigma (PROFILE - BR); (2) the signal concentrates exactly in the predicted regime — zero hero-delta in the 7 bias-inactive leaves where BR == blueprint by construction, growing to top-8-by-|delta| concentrated at late streets with 2.4 active pairs/leaf; (3) BR selects non-blueprint correctly on 94% of resolved pairs with a non-degenerate menu distribution (biases 1/2/3 each picked multiple times). The architecture is firing as designed.
